frameworks.name: directed vs random exploration
frameworks.one_line: two distinct exploration strategies modulating uncertainty-driven choice and choice stochasticity

frameworks.name: exploration-exploitation trade-off
frameworks.one_line: agents must balance choosing known high-reward options against gathering information

frameworks.name: dopaminergic neurogenetics of decision-making
frameworks.one_line: prefrontal vs striatal dopamine variants linked to distinct exploration components

core_question: Do dopamine-related genetic polymorphisms predict individual differences in directed versus random exploration strategies?
key_assumptions: directed and random exploration are dissociable strategies
the optimal exploration-exploitation solution is intractable
SNPs in COMT and DARPP-32 index prefrontal and striatal dopamine function respectively
manipulations: task independently varies uncertainty driving directed exploration vs random exploration
genotype variation at COMT and DARPP-32 loci across participants
key_findings: COMT variation predicts degree of directed exploration and overall choice randomness
DARPP-32 variation predicts both directed and random exploration
individual differences in exploration strategies covary with dopaminergic genotype
interpretations: dopamine makes multiple contributions to exploration depending on afferent target
prefrontal and striatal dopamine systems differentially support exploration components
